title: How Payment Methods for Filipino Digital Creators Works and How to Set It Up Today
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: Selling digital products from Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, Pakistan, or Bangladesh is possible with crypto-native infrastructure that has no geographic restrictions.

content.text: If you're a Filipino digital creator struggling to sell ebooks, courses, or other digital products online due to the strict policies of PayPal or other payment processors, you're not alone. Many platforms restrict creators in the Philippines from receiving payments, leaving you with limited options. But what if you could bypass these restrictions and sell your digital products to anyone worldwide, without worrying about payment processing headaches? Today, we'll show you how to break free from these limitations and start selling your digital products globally using a blockchain-based payment method.
Why This Method Works When Everything Else Fails

This method works because it uses blockchain technology to enable instant and secure transactions, eliminating the need for intermediaries like PayPal or Stripe. By using NOWPayments, a payment processor that supports over 300 coins, you can accept payments from anywhere in the world, without worrying about geographic restrictions or KYC requirements. This means you can focus on creating and selling your digital products, without the hassle of payment processing.
What You Need Before You Start

Before you start selling your digital products, you'll need to sign up for a NOWPayments account, which is free and requires no KYC. You'll also need to set up your Web3 Wallet Connect frontend integration, which costs $19.99 and provides a user-friendly interface for buyers to pay in their preferred cryptocurrency. Finally, you'll need to integrate your Core Shop Engine backend, which automates delivery and payment verification, and provides PDF receipts for your buyers.
Setting Up Your Payment and Delivery Pipeline

To set up your payment and delivery pipeline, you'll need to integrate your Web3 Wallet Connect frontend with your Core Shop Engine backend. This will enable instant automated delivery of your digital products, as well as real-time payment verification. You'll also need to set up your Admin Dashboard, which provides transaction logs, order management, and real-time analytics. With this setup, you'll be able to track your sales, manage your orders, and analyze your performance in real-time.
Blockchain-based payment methods can bypass geographic restrictions and KYC requirements, enabling international digital sales.
Going Live and Making Your First Sale

Once you've set up your payment and delivery pipeline, it's time to go live and make your first sale. To do this, simply upload your digital products to your Core Shop Engine backend, and share your Web3 Wallet Connect frontend with your buyers. When a buyer makes a payment, your Core Shop Engine backend will automatically deliver the product, and your Admin Dashboard will update in real-time. With this setup, you'll be able to sell your digital products to anyone, anywhere in the world, without worrying about payment processing headaches.
Scaling Once the System Is Running

Once your system is running, you can scale your business by adding more digital products, increasing your marketing efforts, and exploring new revenue streams. With your Core Shop Engine backend and Web3 Wallet Connect frontend in place, you'll be able to handle increased traffic and sales without worrying about payment processing bottlenecks. Simply focus on creating and selling your digital products, and let your Core Shop Engine backend and Admin Dashboard take care of the rest.
How can creators in Africa sell digital products internationally?

The most practical path for creators in restricted countries is a combination of NOWPayments for payment processing and USDT on Tron for the primary payment coin. USDT is a stable coin pegged to the US dollar, which means your revenue does not fluctuate with crypto market movements. Tron network fees are under one dollar, and settlement takes under 60 seconds. Once you receive USDT in your wallet, you convert to local currency through P2P exchanges — Binance P2P, Paxful, or local platforms specific to your country. The conversion typically takes 20-30 minutes and costs 2-5% of the transaction, which is significantly less than PayPal's international transfer fees or Western Union's rates. This is the complete payment flow that thousands of creators in Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, Pakistan, and Bangladesh are using right now.
NOWPayments supports over 300 coins, eliminating the need for intermediaries like PayPal or Stripe.
